title: Agent Arena
summary: A file-first arcade intelligence engine for remixable AI-agent competition concepts.

c1.title: KPMG rolls Claude across 276K+ workforce
c1.rationale: KPMG gives Claude a tax, legal, cyber, and private-equity deployment proof point with global workforce reach.

c1.title: Anthropic acquires Stainless
c1.rationale: Stainless strengthens SDK, CLI, and MCP server tooling: exactly the connective tissue agents need.

c2.title: OpenAI x Dell targets hybrid Codex
c2.rationale: Dell packaging pushes Codex toward enterprise data-center and on-prem workflows instead of only cloud delegation.

c2.title: Codex becomes generally available
c2.rationale: GA turns Codex from research-preview hype into a broadly sellable OpenAI developer workflow.

c1.title: PwC expands Claude deployment
c1.rationale: PwC gives Claude a finance, deals, and enterprise-transformation lane with training/certification scale.

c2.title: OpenAI ships Codex workflow upgrades
c2.rationale: Codex upgrades reinforce the thesis that OpenAI is converging CLI, ChatGPT, GitHub, and app workflows.

c1.title: Claude connects to CoCounsel Legal
c1.rationale: The Thomson Reuters partnership makes Claude legible to lawyers through an existing legal work surface.

c2.title: OpenAI responds to TanStack supply-chain attack
c2.rationale: The response makes Codex distribution and desktop trust part of the product battlefield.

c1.title: Anthropic expands Amazon compute deal
c1.rationale: AWS/Trainium capacity gives Claude a durability stat: enough infrastructure to keep serving and training at scale.

c1.title: Mythos / Project Glasswing appears
c1.rationale: Mythos turns Claude security capability into a coordinated defender narrative instead of a pure model benchmark.

c2.title: Codex Security enters preview
c2.rationale: Codex Security gives OpenAI a threat-model, validation, and patching move for enterprise security buyers.

c1.rationale: Claude is strong with Claude Code, Bun, Stainless, MCP, and Labs, especially where teams want agentic coding tied to enterprise trust.

c2.rationale: Codex leads developers through OpenAI distribution, Codex web/CLI, GitHub workflows, GA momentum, and the Rust-heavy open-source harness.

c1.rationale: Claude leads legal through Thomson Reuters/CoCounsel Legal and KPMG tax/legal workflows, where accuracy and trust are explicit selling points.

c2.rationale: Codex legal score is mostly an enterprise-platform spillover rather than a dedicated legal launch in the baseline sources.

c1.rationale: Claude is lifted by KPMG private-equity/tax work, PwC deals and finance expertise, and Anthropic enterprise positioning.

c2.rationale: Codex is credible in finance through OpenAI enterprise distribution and developer automation, but the current evidence is more general than finance-specific.

c1.rationale: Claude healthcare is an enterprise-trust and safety-adjacent inference from Anthropic positioning, not a top current launch vector in the baseline.

c2.rationale: Codex healthcare is an enterprise-distribution inference from OpenAI business reach rather than a dedicated Codex healthcare wedge.

c1.rationale: Claude education benefits from careful reasoning and work-assistant positioning, but the current evidence set is not education-led.

c2.rationale: Codex and OpenAI have stronger broad education/developer mindshare through ChatGPT access, Codex availability, and public developer workflows.

c1.rationale: Claude creative score is lifted by Claude for Creative Work, connectors, and Claude Design from Labs.

c2.rationale: Codex creative score comes from OpenAI distribution and app-building workflows, but this row is less Codex-specific than developer territory.

c1.rationale: Claude customer-ops score reflects enterprise workflow positioning, Claude in Slack/Microsoft surfaces, and professional-services deployment proof.

c2.rationale: Codex customer-ops score rides OpenAI application distribution and automation workflows across ChatGPT Business and developer surfaces.

c1.rationale: Claude government score is boosted by Anthropic safety framing, compliance posture, Mythos/Glasswing defensive coalition, and regulated-work evidence.

c2.rationale: Codex government score is constrained by a developer-agent focus, with security and safe execution helping but less government-specific public proof.

c1.rationale: Claude enterprise IT score reflects KPMG, PwC, Claude Code revenue, MCP, Stainless, AWS capacity, and trust-heavy enterprise posture.

c2.rationale: Codex enterprise IT score reflects Simplex, Dell hybrid/on-prem, Codex Security, GA, and OpenAI capital/compute distribution.

c1.rationale: Claude science score is boosted by frontier research posture, Karpathy joining pre-training, Opus 4.7, and Mythos security research.

c2.rationale: Codex science score reflects OpenAI research depth, Codex-optimized models, internal SWE task benchmarks, and compute infrastructure.

c1.rationale: Claude momentum is driven by Anthropic Labs, the Claude Code revenue milestone, recent KPMG/PwC enterprise alliances, Stainless, and Mythos/Glasswing security theater.

c2.rationale: Codex momentum is driven by GA, workflow upgrades, Simplex adoption, Dell enterprise packaging, Codex Security, and the open-source CLI/repo loop.

c1.rationale: Claude mindshare is concentrated in safety, enterprise trust, legal/tax/professional-services workflows, and Claude Code practitioners rather than broad consumer distribution.

c2.rationale: Codex mindshare rides OpenAI distribution, ChatGPT placement, the public Codex launch/GA sequence, GitHub-visible open source, and developer workflow coverage.

c1.rationale: Claude enterprise score reflects KPMG, PwC, Thomson Reuters legal, Anthropic partner motion, and AWS/Trainium capacity for scaled customer demand.

c2.rationale: Codex enterprise score reflects Simplex, ChatGPT Business/Enterprise access, Dell hybrid/on-prem positioning, and OpenAI capital/compute leverage.

c1.rationale: Claude ship score is boosted by Labs, Claude Code, Skills, MCP, Stainless, Bun, Claude Design, and security/legal vertical surfaces.

c2.rationale: Codex ship score is boosted by Codex GA, upgrades, the CLI/open-source harness, SDK/workflow integrations, Codex Security, and frequent app-surface expansion.

sources.title: Company
notes: Anthropic describes itself as an AI safety and research company building reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.
The company page lists board and long-term benefit trust governance context.

sources.title: Introducing Labs
notes: Anthropic expanded Labs to incubate experimental products at the frontier of Claude capabilities.
The announcement says Mike Krieger moved into Labs with Ben Mann, and Ami Vora would lead Product with Rahul Patil.

sources.title: OpenAI raises $122 billion to accelerate the next phase of AI
notes: OpenAI announced $122 billion in committed capital at an $852 billion post-money valuation.
The announcement positions consumer adoption, enterprise deployment, developer usage, and compute as a reinforcing flywheel.

sources.title: Simplex rethinks software development with Codex
notes: OpenAI published an enterprise case study where Simplex selected Codex as its primary coding agent.
The case study frames agentic systems as moving beyond assistive coding into delegated development work.

sources.title: Our response to the TanStack npm supply chain attack
notes: OpenAI warned users to avoid unexpected OpenAI, ChatGPT, or Codex installers from third-party links.
The response adds a security/comms signal around Codex distribution and desktop app trust.

sources.title: OpenAI co-founder Andrej Karpathy joins Anthropic
notes: Axios reported that Andrej Karpathy joined Anthropic and would start on the pre-training team.
The report frames the hire as a major talent win in frontier AI.

sources.title: Introductions
notes: Jason Liu says he is a Developer Experience Engineer on the Codex team at OpenAI.
He describes himself as the creator of Instructor, a Python library for structured LLM outputs.

sources.title: Why Instructor is the Best Library for Structured LLM Outputs
notes: The Instructor article explains mapping LLM outputs into Pydantic models through a response_model parameter.
It emphasizes low-abstraction, SDK-compatible structured output workflows.

sources.title: LLM engineering course: Master structured outputs and data validation
notes: Weights & Biases lists Jason Liu as course instructor and creator of the Instructor library.
The course focuses on structured outputs and data validation.

sources.title: OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger joins OpenAI
notes: TechCrunch reported that Peter Steinberger, creator of OpenClaw, joined OpenAI.
The report says Sam Altman framed the role around the next generation of personal agents.

sources.title: The creator of OpenClaw used $1,300,000+ of OpenAI tokens in 30 days
notes: PC Gamer reported on Steinberger's posted OpenAI usage: 603 billion tokens across 7.6 million requests.
The report links the usage to around 100 Codex instances and continued OpenClaw development.

sources.title: Assessing Claude Mythos Preview cybersecurity capabilities
notes: Project Glasswing frames Mythos as a defensive security coalition and a major Claude capability unlock.

sources.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
notes: Anthropic positions Opus 4.7 as stronger across coding, agents, vision, and multi-step work.

sources.title: Making frontier cybersecurity capabilities available to defenders
notes: Claude Code Security is treated as a defensive agentic security product signal.

sources.title: Anthropic acquires Bun as Claude Code reaches $1B milestone
notes: Anthropic says Claude Code reached a $1B run-rate milestone and that Bun strengthens AI-led software engineering infrastructure.

sources.title: Anthropic acquires Stainless
notes: Anthropic says Stainless strengthens SDK and MCP server tooling for agent connectivity.

sources.title: Anthropic and Amazon expand collaboration for up to 5 gigawatts of new compute
notes: Anthropic says the Amazon agreement secures up to 5GW of capacity for training and deploying Claude.

sources.title: Thomson Reuters and Anthropic expand partnership to connect Claude with CoCounsel Legal
notes: Thomson Reuters frames Claude as connected to legal workflows through CoCounsel Legal.

sources.title: KPMG integrates Claude across its core business and workforce
notes: Anthropic says KPMG is rolling Claude out across 276,000+ employees and client work including tax, legal, cybersecurity, and private equity.

sources.title: PwC is deploying Claude to build technology, execute deals, and reinvent enterprise functions
notes: Anthropic and PwC announced Claude Code, Cowork, a Center of Excellence, and certification for 30,000 PwC professionals.

sources.title: Claude for Creative Work
notes: Anthropic announced creative-work connectors and positioned Claude Design as a Labs product.

sources.title: Introducing Codex
notes: OpenAI introduced Codex as a cloud software engineering agent and open-source terminal CLI.

sources.title: Codex is now generally available
notes: OpenAI made Codex generally available and expanded its workflow surface.

sources.title: Introducing upgrades to Codex
notes: OpenAI announced Codex workflow upgrades across developer surfaces.

sources.title: Unrolling the Codex agent loop
notes: OpenAI explains Codex as an agent loop for planning, editing, running commands, and reporting evidence.

sources.title: Codex Security: now in research preview
notes: OpenAI says Codex Security scans repositories, builds threat models, validates findings, and proposes fixes.

sources.title: Running Codex safely at OpenAI
notes: OpenAI describes internal operating practices for running Codex safely.

sources.title: Building the compute infrastructure for the Intelligence Age
notes: OpenAI frames Stargate and related infrastructure as a compute platform for frontier AI.

sources.title: openai/codex
notes: The openai/codex repository exposes the local Codex agent implementation and language mix.

sources.title: OpenAI and Dell Technologies partner to bring Codex to hybrid and on-premises enterprise environments
notes: OpenAI and Dell position Codex for hybrid and on-prem enterprise environments.

integrations.title: X Watchlist
integrations.description: Monitors official and key-person X accounts for evidence-backed signals.

surfaces.title: Reference Dashboard
surfaces.description: The default Next.js rendering surface for arena snapshots.

surfaces.title: Extension Lab
surfaces.description: Generic renderer for dormant and experimental arena concepts.

lists.name: AI
lists.description: Existing mixed Codex and Claude Code watchlist

scoringRubric.disclaimer: Arcade scores are narrative indices, not scientific benchmarks. Each number should point to a public-source rationale.

characterStats.ATK: Product or research offensive pressure: launches, model strength, public agenda-setting, or concrete builder leverage.
characterStats.DEF: Safety, trust, compliance, security, governance, and ability to absorb scrutiny.
characterStats.SPD: Shipping speed and signal velocity: how quickly the person or surface turns capability into visible motion.
characterStats.COMBO: Platform leverage: integrations, ecosystem effects, workflow chaining, and ability to compound with other moves.
characterStats.HP: Durability: capital, compute, org depth, adoption, technical moat, and staying power.

teamPower.momentum: Recent public signals plus strategic weight of launches, hires, partnerships, and adoption proof.
teamPower.mindshare: Share of attention in developer, enterprise, and public product narratives.
teamPower.enterprise: Evidence of paid business workflows, regulated-industry adoption, partnerships, and deployment credibility.
teamPower.ship: Visible product releases, workflow surface expansion, and cadence of concrete shipping moves.

scoringRubric.verticals: Vertical conquest blends public product fit, enterprise proof, partner distribution, and inferred adoption pressure. Close scores are intentionally contested.
